message:
fix(wamr): Force rebuild on config change via marker file

The WAMR cache was being restored with the old SIMDE=1 config,
and the build script was skipping the rebuild because libiwasm.a
already existed. Now we use a config marker file to detect when
the configuration has changed and force a rebuild.

- Add CONFIG_KEY and CONFIG_MARKER for config validation
- Check config matches before skipping build
- Delete build dir and rebuild if config changed
- Write marker file after successful build

files.patch:
@@ -16,10 +16,24 @@ SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
 REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
 BUILD_DIR="${REPO_ROOT}/vendor/wamr/product-mini/platforms/${PLATFORM}/build"
 
-# Skip if already built
-if [ -f "${BUILD_DIR}/libiwasm.a" ]; then
-    echo "WAMR already built at ${BUILD_DIR}/libiwasm.a"
-    exit 0
+# Determine configuration key (used to detect config changes)
+if [[ "$PLATFORM" == "linux" && "$ARCH" == "x86_64" ]]; then
+    CONFIG_KEY="linux-x64-simd-native-v3"
+else
+    CONFIG_KEY="${PLATFORM}-${ARCH}-simd-simde-v3"
+fi
+CONFIG_MARKER="${BUILD_DIR}/.wamr_config"
+
+# Skip if already built with matching config
+if [ -f "${BUILD_DIR}/libiwasm.a" ] && [ -f "${CONFIG_MARKER}" ]; then
+    EXISTING_CONFIG=$(cat "${CONFIG_MARKER}")
+    if [ "$EXISTING_CONFIG" == "$CONFIG_KEY" ]; then
+        echo "WAMR already built with config: ${CONFIG_KEY}"
+        exit 0
+    else
+        echo "Config changed from ${EXISTING_CONFIG} to ${CONFIG_KEY}, rebuilding..."
+        rm -rf "${BUILD_DIR}"
+    fi
 fi
 
 echo "Building WAMR for ${PLATFORM} (${ARCH})..."
@@ -49,4 +63,7 @@ fi
 # Build with all available cores
 make -j$(nproc 2>/dev/null || sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || echo 4)
 
-echo "WAMR built successfully at ${BUILD_DIR}/libiwasm.a"
+# Write config marker for cache validation
+echo "${CONFIG_KEY}" > "${CONFIG_MARKER}"
+
+echo "WAMR built successfully at ${BUILD_DIR}/libiwasm.a (config: ${CONFIG_KEY})"
